scenario_outcomes.notes: Defined bounded intake workflow with ownership, risk triggers, and clear routing conditions.
Mapped elevated-risk use case to approval gates, oversight, and documentation without issuing legal finality.
Required model and system documentation gaps were identified and prioritized.
Third-party model review stayed evidence-backed and explicit about vendor-information limits.
Human oversight checkpoints and escalation responsibilities were clearly tied to risk conditions.
Harm incident handling included containment, evidence capture, rollback review, and governance reapproval.
Monitoring guidance tied thresholds and owners to governance review actions.
Data-quality and privacy dependencies were handled through boundary-safe escalation.
Platform comparison stayed within governance controls and cited authoritative public sources.
Low-confidence case refused to guess and requested missing artifact evidence explicitly.
Adjacent product, engineering, and infrastructure asks were rejected clearly and routed out.
Shared-meta-agent fallback behavior remained conservative and documented refresh limitations.

notes: Scenario coverage includes normal design and implementation guidance, governance handling with data quality dependencies, model failure modes, evidence-backed recommendations, out-of-scope rejection, and low-confidence escalation.
Pass rate 1.00 meets the strict-profile threshold of 0.92 and no unreviewed regressions were identified during manual package review.
Human verification is recorded in readiness evidence and the package includes specialist-owned versus delegated shared-meta-agent contracts as required by issue #105.
